assumptions: The developer has Python 3.10+ and a package manager such as uv or pip.
No LangChain project packages are installed yet for this app.
No model-provider credentials required by the selected quickstart tab exist yet.

developer_goal: Install LangChain, configure one supported chat-model provider, and run the official weather-tool agent until it calls the tool and prints final content blocks.

first_success.outcome: A LangChain agent with a selected chat model and get_weather tool answers the San Francisco weather question after calling the tool.
first_success.observable_signal: The executed example prints the agent's final content blocks and the agent calls get_weather with San Francisco.

route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete install-to-agent-run route only after one model provider and one package-manager path are fixed.

candidates.name: uv install + OpenAI provider tab
candidates.reason: Equal provider tabs are documented; OpenAI onboarding itself is outside LangChain docs.

candidates.name: uv or pip install + Gemini, Anthropic, Ollama, or other provider tab
candidates.reason: Each provider changes account, billing, env vars, and sometimes local daemon requirements with no named default.

candidates.name: LangSmith tracing-first journey
candidates.reason: Overview recommends LangSmith for debugging, but the agent quickstart reaches first success without requiring a LangSmith account.

required_path.action: Create or use credentials for one supported model provider required by the selected quickstart tab.

required_fields.label: Provider account and API key fields
required_fields.notes: LangChain documents env var names only; each provider's signup path is outside these pages.

required_path.observable_result: A provider API key or local runtime credential exists for the chosen model string.

required_path.action: Initialize the project and install langchain and deepagents with the selected package manager.

required_fields.label: Install commands
required_fields.notes: uv init/add/sync and pip variants are both documented.

required_path.observable_result: LangChain and deepagents are installed in the project environment.

required_path.action: Export the environment variable or variables required by the chosen provider.
required_path.observable_result: The selected model provider credential is available to the process.

required_path.action: Create runnable Python that imports create_agent, defines get_weather, builds the agent, and invokes the San Francisco weather message.
required_path.observable_result: The example file is ready to execute.

required_path.action: Run the example and print the final message content blocks.
required_path.observable_result: The agent calls get_weather for San Francisco and prints final content blocks.

prerequisites.description: Python 3.10+ and one documented package-management workflow.

external_gates.description: Most hosted model providers require their own account, key creation, and possibly billing before the agent can run.
Local Ollama routes require a running Ollama runtime instead of a hosted key.

platform_outcomes.description: The selected chat model returns content that the agent uses after the weather tool call.

excluded.item: Reading overview comparisons of LangGraph and Deep Agents
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is excluded from required actions.

excluded.item: LangSmith account creation and tracing setup
excluded.reason: Optional observability relative to the agent quickstart first success.

excluded.item: Installing the LangChain Docs MCP server or Skills
excluded.reason: Presented as assistance for coding agents, not required for the manual quickstart.

sources.title: Create an account and API key
evidence_supported: LangSmith signup options for optional tracing journeys

uncertainties.question: Which supported model provider and package-manager path define the canonical LangChain first mile?
uncertainties.impact: Provider onboarding can dominate action and field counts; local Ollama versus hosted keys are not comparable without a fixed choice.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: An explicit workshop or vendor-recommended provider default, then a verified provider onboarding path joined to the LangChain quickstart.

uncertainties.question: Does the measured journey begin at a LangChain/LangSmith account or only at an external model-provider account?
uncertainties.impact: LangChain OSS itself has no required account, which conflicts with a universal account-creation boundary unless the provider account is treated as the start.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A corpus decision that either scopes LangChain as library-only with a named provider, or measures LangSmith signup as the platform account.
